nodes.label: AI as productivity tool for research and editing
nodes.label_negative: AI as creative decision-maker or plotter
nodes.grounding_span: I'm definitely in the driver's seat. It wouldn't work for me if I ever felt that AI was anything other than a productivity tool.

nodes.label: AI as research assistant for technical questions
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or unhelpful for creative suggestions
nodes.grounding_span: I simply asked what the differences might be and was then given a list of changes.

nodes.label: AI as mechanical style translator
nodes.grounding_span: write a scene in first person present... then let AI shift it over to 3rd person past

nodes.label: AI as market threat via derivative content
nodes.grounding_span: people out there using AI to crank out massive numbers of derivative books each year

nodes.label: Creative autonomy and control
nodes.grounding_span: I'm definitely in the driver's seat

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: saves me a good bit of time because I stay in the flow

nodes.label: Economic sustainability for writers
nodes.grounding_span: flooding the market with derivative AI content is going to make it harder to earn a living

nodes.label: Positive stance toward AI as research and editing assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I love the idea of AI as a research and editing assistant

nodes.label: Negative stance toward AI for creative decisions
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't be including to use AI for plotting or outlining

nodes.label: Concern about AI-generated derivative content
nodes.grounding_span: writers (including me) are worried that flooding the market with derivative AI content

nodes.label: Pantser writing style — intuitive, discovery-driven
nodes.grounding_span: My writing style is commonly known as 'pantser' as in 'flying by the seat of your pants.'

nodes.label: Pragmatic boundary-setting — clear separation of AI tasks from creative core
nodes.grounding_span: I'm definitely in the driver's seat. It wouldn't work for me if I ever felt that AI was anything other than a productivity tool.
